exchanges.question: May I know your name?
exchanges.good_reply: Nice! Can you introduce yourself please? 😊
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Yes, you may. My name is [name].

exchanges.question: Could you introduce your friend to me?
exchanges.good_reply: What is her name? (for a girl) / What is his name? (for a boy) 👤
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Yes I could, this is Mr./Ms. [name].

exchanges.question: What is her name?
exchanges.good_reply: What is his name? 👤
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Her name is [name].

exchanges.question: What is his name?
exchanges.good_reply: I am glad/happy/delighted/pleased to meet you! 🎉
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: His name is [name].

exchanges.question: How do you say 'Enchanté' in English?
expected_answers: it has been nice meeting you
it was a big pleasure to know you
exchanges.good_reply: Excellent! You've mastered introductions! 🎯
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Nice to meet you, or Me too.

exchanges.question: What are your feelings about WEC-BEC?
exchanges.good_reply: Are you happy to be here at WEC-BEC? 🏆
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am very happy, or I am so excited.

exchanges.question: Are you happy to be here at WEC-BEC?
exchanges.good_reply: Me too! Let's continue learning! 📚
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Of course, I am so happy to be here.

exchanges.question: What do you mean by that?
exchanges.good_reply: What does pawpaw mean in French? 🍈
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I mean [explanation].

exchanges.question: What does pawpaw mean in French?
exchanges.good_reply: What is the meaning of 'host family' in French? 🏠
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Pawpaw means 'la papaye' in French.

exchanges.question: What is the meaning of 'host family' in French?
exchanges.good_reply: How do you say 'le riz' in English? 🍚
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: The meaning of host family in French is 'famille d'accueil'.

exchanges.question: How do you say 'le riz' in English?
exchanges.good_reply: What does WEC-BEC stand for? 🏫
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: It is rice in English.

exchanges.question: What does WEC-BEC stand for?
exchanges.good_reply: Perfect! You understand translations well! 🌟
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: WEC-BEC stands for Foreign Languages Training Center.

exchanges.question: How do you say 'fat' in French?
exchanges.good_reply: Great job! Keep asking questions! 🦋
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: It is 'gros' in French.

conversations.title: 📅 Lesson 10: There is / There was / There will be

exchanges.question: Can you tell me about the different uses of 'il y a', 'il y avait', and 'il y aura'?
expected_answers: present tense there is there are
past tense there was there were
exchanges.good_reply: Give me an example with 'there is'! ✨
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Explain: Present = there is/are, Past = there was/were, Future = there will be.

conversations.title: 🔤 Lesson 11: Indefinite Article: A and An

exchanges.question: What's the difference between 'a' and 'an'?
exchanges.good_reply: Perfect! Give me an example with 'a'. 📝
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: We use 'a' before consonants and 'an' before vowels.

exchanges.question: What's the dichotomy between 'at' and 'to'?
exchanges.good_reply: Correct! Give me an example with 'at'. 🏠
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: 'At' is for no movement, 'To' is for movement.

exchanges.question: When do we use the possessive case?
exchanges.good_reply: How do you say 'the house of Greg' using possessive case? 🏡
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: We use possessive case to express possession or ownership.

exchanges.question: How do you say 'the house of Greg' in possessive case?
exchanges.good_reply: What about plural? How do you say 'the house of the Gregs'? 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Greg's house.

exchanges.question: How do you say 'the house of the Gregs' in possessive case (plural)?
exchanges.good_reply: Excellent! You've mastered possessive case! 🎉
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. For plural, put apostrophe after 's': Gregs' house.

exchanges.question: What time is it? / What time do you make it? / What time do you have? / What's the time?
exchanges.good_reply: Perfect! Now you can tell time in English! 🕐
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: It's [number] o'clock, or It's [minutes] past [hour].

exchanges.question: How did you spend your night?
exchanges.good_reply: What was our last lesson about? 📚
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I spent an awful / good night.

exchanges.question: What is a decade?
exchanges.good_reply: What is a century? 📅
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: A decade is a period of ten years.

exchanges.question: What is a century?
expected_answers: a period of one hundred years
exchanges.good_reply: How many seasons are there in Europe? 🌍
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: A century is a period of one hundred years.

exchanges.question: How many seasons are there in Europe?
exchanges.good_reply: How many seasons are there in central Africa? ☀️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: There are 4 seasons in Europe: Winter, Autumn, Spring, Summer.

exchanges.question: How many seasons are there in central Africa?
exchanges.good_reply: Great revision! Keep practicing! 🎯
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: There are 2 seasons in central Africa: dry season and rain season.

exchanges.question: What is your full name?
exchanges.good_reply: How do you spell your name? 🔤
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My full name is [first name + last name].

exchanges.question: How do you spell your name?
exchanges.good_reply: What is your email address? 📧
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I spell my name [letters].

exchanges.question: What is your email address?
exchanges.good_reply: What is your dwelling place / address? 🏠
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My email address is [email].

exchanges.question: What is your phone number?
exchanges.good_reply: Perfect! Now I can contact you! 📱
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My phone number is [number].

exchanges.question: Where are you coming from now?
expected_answers: i am coming from the center
exchanges.good_reply: Where were you? 📍
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am coming from [place].

exchanges.question: Where were you? / Where have you been?
expected_answers: i have been at my sister's house
exchanges.good_reply: What is your nationality? 🌍
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I was at [place].

exchanges.question: What is your nationality?
exchanges.good_reply: Where do you come from? 🗺️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am [nationality] by birth.

exchanges.question: Where do you hail from?
exchanges.good_reply: What is your ethnic group / tribe? 👥
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I hail from [country].

exchanges.question: How old are you? / What's your age?
expected_answers: i am a twenty year old boy
i am a twenty year old girl
i am eighteen years of age
exchanges.good_reply: What's the age gap between you and your brother? 👨‍👦
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am [number] years old.

exchanges.question: What's the age gap between you and your brother?
expected_answers: we have the age gap of three years
exchanges.good_reply: Great! Now I know your age! 🎉
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: We have the age gap of [number] years.

conversations.title: 📅 Lesson 19: Date and Place of Birth

exchanges.question: When were you born?
exchanges.good_reply: Where were you born? 🏥
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I was born in [year].

exchanges.question: Where were you born?
exchanges.good_reply: When is your birthday? 🎂
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I was born in [city].

exchanges.question: When is your birthday?
exchanges.good_reply: Where did you grow up? 🌱
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My birthday is in [month].

exchanges.question: Who brought you up?
exchanges.good_reply: Thank you for sharing! 💕
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My [grand mother/father/parents] brought me up.

exchanges.question: What position are you in your family?
expected_answers: i am the last but one
exchanges.good_reply: How many are you in your family? 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am the eldest, or I am the last, or I am the last but one.

exchanges.question: What is your marital status?
exchanges.good_reply: Are you engaged to someone? 💑
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am single / married / divorced.

exchanges.question: Are you engaged to someone?
exchanges.good_reply: Have you got a boyfriend or girlfriend? 💕
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am engaged to [name], or No, I am not.

exchanges.question: Have you got a boyfriend or girlfriend?
exchanges.good_reply: Are you faithful to your partner? 💖
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Yes I have, or No I haven't, I am free.

conversations.title: 📆 Lesson 22: Days, Weeks, Months and Dates

exchanges.question: How many days are there in the week?
exchanges.good_reply: How many weeks are there in the month? 📅
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: There are seven days in the week.

exchanges.question: How many weeks are there in the month?
exchanges.good_reply: How many months are there in the year? 🗓️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: There are 4 weeks in the month.

exchanges.question: What is the date today? / What's today's date?
exchanges.good_reply: What was the date yesterday? ⏪
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Today is [day, month date].

exchanges.question: What will be the date tomorrow?
exchanges.good_reply: Perfect! You understand dates! 📅
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Tomorrow will be [day, month date].

exchanges.question: What do you do during your holidays / vacations / free time / day off?
exchanges.good_reply: That sounds fun! 🎉
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: During my holidays I travel, or During my free time I read.

exchanges.question: Whose bag is this?
exchanges.good_reply: Who is the owner of this house? 🏠
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: It is mine, or It belongs to me.

exchanges.question: To whom does this pen belong?
exchanges.good_reply: Perfect! You've mastered possession! 🔑
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: It belongs to me, or It is mine.

exchanges.question: What do you like and dislike? / What are your likes and dislikes?
expected_answers: i like eating and dislike starving
i like fruits and i dislike vegetables
exchanges.good_reply: What is your favorite dish? 🍽️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I like [something] and dislike [something].

exchanges.question: What is your favorite dish?
exchanges.good_reply: Who is your idol / best artist in music? 🎵
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My favorite dish is [food].

exchanges.question: What is your pet aversion?
exchanges.good_reply: Good to know your preferences! 🌟
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I hate [something].

exchanges.question: Where is your center located / situated?
expected_answers: our center is located at thomas
exchanges.good_reply: What's the time table of your center? 🕐
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Our center is located at [place].

exchanges.question: How often do you study per week?
exchanges.good_reply: Is there any library in your school? 📚
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: We study [number] times a week.

exchanges.question: Which university do you attend?
exchanges.good_reply: What is your department at the university? 📖
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I attend [university name].

exchanges.question: What is your level / grade?
expected_answers: i am in the first level
i am in the first form of primary school
i am in the first level of junior secondary school
i am in the first grade of senior secondary school
exchanges.good_reply: Have you moved up to the next class? ⬆️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am in [level/grade].

exchanges.question: What is your major / speciality?
exchanges.good_reply: What have you majored in at the university? 🏫
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My major is [subject].

exchanges.question: What's your section at school?
exchanges.good_reply: What are your qualifications? 🎓
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am in [letter] section.

exchanges.question: When will be your graduation?
expected_answers: my graduation will take place on monday
exchanges.good_reply: Congratulations in advance! 🎉
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My graduation will take place on [day].

exchanges.question: What's your grade? / What year are you?
expected_answers: i am in my first year
exchanges.good_reply: Do you have a B.A or M.A? 📜
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am a freshman/sophomore/junior/senior.

conversations.title: 🎯 Lesson 29: To Expect, To Aim, Goal and Motivation

exchanges.question: What do you expect / target / aim / purpose / intend / plan / want to do in life?
expected_answers: i expect to be a healer
i target to be a pastor
i aim to be a business man
i purpose to be a boss
i intend to be a barrister
i plan to become a trader
i want to be a healer
exchanges.good_reply: What would you like to do after studies? 🎓
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I expect to be a [profession].

exchanges.question: What is your goal / target / aim / purpose / intention / objective / plan / vision in life?
expected_answers: my target is to be a pastor
my aim is to become a boss
my purpose is to go overseas
my intention is to be trader
my objective is to be a pastor
my plan is to go abroad
my vision is to travel a lot
exchanges.good_reply: What motivated you to learn English? 💪
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My goal is to [action].

exchanges.question: What motivated you to learn English? / What pushed you to learn English? / What urged you to learn English?
exchanges.good_reply: Excellent motivation! Keep going! 🚀
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I want to travel abroad, or for work reasons.

exchanges.question: What is your hobby / leisure / pastime / spare time / distraction / diversion / entertainment / interest / game?
exchanges.good_reply: Great! Hobbies are important for relaxation! 🎯
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My hobby is [activity].

exchanges.question: Are you monolingual, bilingual, trilingual, quadrilingual or multilingual?
exchanges.good_reply: Can you express yourself in English? 💬
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am bilingual / trilingual / multilingual.

exchanges.question: Can you speak English?
expected_answers: i don't know how to speak english
exchanges.good_reply: What is the secret of learning? 🔑
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am still a beginner, or I know nothing about English.

exchanges.question: What is the secret of learning?
exchanges.good_reply: What is the importance of learning many languages? 🌍
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: You must read a lot.

exchanges.question: What is the relevance or importance of learning many languages?
expected_answers: you will easily have a job
exchanges.good_reply: Can you convey a message in English now? 📨
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: If you know many languages, you will easily have a job.

conversations.title: 🔍 Lesson 32: To Look For and To Wait For

exchanges.question: Who are you looking for?
exchanges.good_reply: What are you looking for? 🔍
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am looking for [person].

exchanges.question: What are you looking for?
expected_answers: i am looking for my wallet
exchanges.good_reply: Who are you waiting for? ⏳
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am looking for [object].

exchanges.question: Who are you waiting for?
expected_answers: i am waiting for my darling
exchanges.good_reply: What are you waiting for? 🕐
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am waiting for [person].

exchanges.question: Who do you rely on? / Who do you depend on?
exchanges.good_reply: Are your parents alive? 👨‍👩‍👧
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I rely on my parents.

exchanges.question: What does your father do?
exchanges.good_reply: What rank is your father? 🎖️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My father is a [profession].

exchanges.question: What is your profession? / What do you do as work?
exchanges.good_reply: Thank you for sharing! 💼
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am a [profession].

exchanges.question: Do you pray?
exchanges.good_reply: Where do you pray? 🕌
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Yes I do, I am a Christian.

exchanges.question: Are you born again?
exchanges.good_reply: Are you a believer? 🙏
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Yes I am, or No I am not.

exchanges.question: What is your religion?
exchanges.good_reply: Does dooms day exist? ⚠️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: My religion is [religion].

conversations.title: ✈️ Lesson 35: To Go Abroad and To Go Overseas

exchanges.question: Have you ever been abroad?
exchanges.good_reply: When will you go abroad? 🗓️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I have never been abroad, or Yes I have.

exchanges.question: Are you hoping to travel overseas?
exchanges.good_reply: Do you hope to live overseas? 🌍
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Yes of course, or No.

exchanges.question: What is going on? / What is happening to you? / What is wrong with you?
exchanges.good_reply: What are you suffering from? 🩺
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am sick, or My leg is paining.

exchanges.question: What are you suffering from?
expected_answers: i am suffering from a headache
exchanges.good_reply: Get well soon! 💊
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I am suffering from a headache.

exchanges.question: Are you a Congolese citizen?
exchanges.good_reply: What is the capital city of Congo? 🏙️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Yes I am.

exchanges.question: What is the capital city of Congo?
exchanges.good_reply: What is the official language in Congo? 🗣️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: The capital of Congo is Brazzaville.

exchanges.question: What are the national languages in Congo?
exchanges.good_reply: What is the population of Congo? 👥
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Lingala and Kituba.

exchanges.question: What are the colors of the Congolese flag?
exchanges.good_reply: Who is the explorer of Congo? 🧭
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Green, yellow, and red.

exchanges.question: What is the Congolese currency?
exchanges.good_reply: Great! You know Congo well! 🇨🇬
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: The currency is CFA.

exchanges.question: What are the advantages and disadvantages of reading a lot?
exchanges.good_reply: What are the pros and cons of love? 💕
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Advantages: you discover things. Disadvantages: you can become crazy.

exchanges.question: Who discovered America?
exchanges.good_reply: Who created the plane? ✈️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Christopher Columbus discovered America in 1492.

exchanges.question: Who created the telephone?
exchanges.good_reply: Who made the atomic bomb? 💣
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Alexander Bell created the telephone.

exchanges.question: Who is the first astronaut?
exchanges.good_reply: Who was the first president of France's Fifth Republic? 🇫🇷
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: Neil Armstrong is the first astronaut.

exchanges.question: Who created the airplane?
exchanges.good_reply: Excellent general knowledge! 🎓
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: The Wright brothers created the airplane.

conversations.title: ⏳ Lesson 40: How long... for, since, and ago

exchanges.question: How long have you been learning English?
exchanges.good_reply: How long ago did you visit Oyo? 🗓️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I have been learning English for [time] or since [date].

exchanges.question: How long ago did you visit Oyo?
exchanges.good_reply: Perfect! You understand 'for', 'since', and 'ago'! ⏰
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: I visited [place] [number] years ago.

exchanges.question: How much is it? / How much money is it? / How much does it cost?
exchanges.good_reply: I am sorry, I am short of money. 💸
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: It is [amount], or It costs [amount].
